svg.title: The cleanup that couldn't delete: a 404 that meant leaked forever
svg.desc: A five-step failure in the sweeper. Step one: the sweeper works out which object to delete by reconstructing the storage path from the image's public URL. Step two: but when the base URL carries a path prefix, the reconstructed path is not the real storage path, so the DELETE targets a path that does not exist. Step three: BunnyCDN returns 404. Step four: the delete method treats 404 as idempotent success — correct in isolation, since already-gone should not be retried — so the row is marked Deleted and the sweeper moves on. Step five: the real file sits on the CDN untouched, now with no tracking row pointing at it — an unrecoverable orphan, the exact failure the whole system exists to prevent, dressed as success. Below, the fix: carry the storage path separately from the public URL so the sweeper deletes what it actually stored. The bug was found in a second PR-review round; the dry-run gate, which logs every would-delete path before any real deletion, is the standing safety net for this class of bug. Hover or focus any step to read it.

g.text: a clean “deleted” log — while the file leaks forever

g.aria-label: Step 1: the sweeper reconstructs the delete path from the image's public URL
g.title: 1 · sweeper derives the delete path from the public URL
g.text: sweeper derives the delete path from the public URL

g.aria-label: Step 2: when the base URL carries a path prefix, the reconstructed path is not the real storage path, so the DELETE targets a path that does not exist
g.title: 2 · base URL prefix → path ≠ real storage path
g.text: base URL has a path prefix → path ≠ real storage path
DELETE targets a path that doesn't exist

g.aria-label: Step 4: the delete method treats 404 as idempotent success — correct in isolation since already-gone should not be retried — so the row is marked Deleted
g.title: 4 · 404 treated as success (idempotent) → row marked Deleted
g.text: 404 treated as success → row marked Deleted
correct in isolation: “already gone” shouldn't retry

g.aria-label: Step 5: the real file sits on the CDN untouched, now with no tracking row pointing at it — an unrecoverable orphan, the exact failure the system exists to prevent, dressed as success
g.title: 5 · the real file leaks forever — an unrecoverable orphan
g.text: the real file leaks forever — no row points at it
the exact failure the system exists to prevent, dressed as success

g.aria-label: The fix: carry the storage path separately from the public URL so the sweeper deletes what it actually stored; the bug was found in a second PR-review round, and the dry-run gate — which logs every would-delete path before any real deletion — is the standing safety net for this class of bug
g.title: fix · carry the storage path separately · found in a 2nd PR review
g.text: fix: carry the storage path separately from the URL
delete what you actually stored — never reconstruct it from the public URL
found in a 2nd PR review · dry-run gate is the standing net before real deletes
an idempotent 404 and a wrong path composed into a silent, unbounded leak
